chapter_title: Edible fruit and nuts; peel of citrus fruit or melons
scope_note: Note 1: Ch.08 does NOT cover INEDIBLE nuts or fruits. Note 2: CHILLED fruits and nuts are classified in the SAME HEADINGS as the corresponding FRESH fruits and nuts (chilled = same heading as fresh). Note 3: DRIED fruit or dried nuts of this Chapter may be PARTIALLY REHYDRATED or TREATED for: (a) additional preservation/stabilisation (moderate heat, sulphuring, sorbic acid/potassium sorbate addition); or (b) to improve/maintain appearance (vegetable oil addition, small quantities of glucose syrup addition) — PROVIDED they RETAIN THE CHARACTER of dried fruit or dried nuts. Such treated dried fruit stays in Ch.08. Note 4: Heading 08.12 applies to fruit/nuts treated SOLELY to ensure provisional preservation during transport/storage (SO2/brine/sulphur water/other preservative solutions) PROVIDED they remain unsuitable for immediate consumption in that state.

inclusions.description: Coconuts, Brazil nuts and cashew nuts, fresh or dried, whether or not shelled or peeled: coconuts (fresh/desiccated coconut flesh → differentiated from coconut oil Ch.15), Brazil nuts in shell/shelled, cashew nuts (raw cashew in shell → 08.01; shelled raw cashew → 08.01; roasted cashew → Ch.20)

Other nuts, fresh or dried, whether or not shelled or peeled: almonds, hazelnuts/filberts, walnuts, chestnuts, pistachios, macadamia, pecans, pine nuts — all in shell or shelled (raw/unprocessed). Excludes roasted/salted nuts
Bananas, including plantains, fresh or dried: dessert bananas, plantains (cooking bananas), dried banana slices
Dates, figs, pineapples, avocados, guavas, mangoes and mangosteens, fresh or dried
Citrus fruit, fresh or dried: oranges, mandarins/clementines/satsumas, lemons, limes, grapefruit, pomelo, citrons — whole fresh fruit
Grapes, fresh or dried (raisins): table grapes, wine grapes (for eating not for wine-making at classification), dried grapes/raisins/sultanas/currants
Apricots, cherries, peaches (including nectarines), plums and sloe plums, fresh
Other fruit, fresh: strawberries, raspberries, blackberries, cranberries, blueberries, kiwifruit, passion fruit, star fruit, lychees, longans, jackfruit, durian, tamarind, pomegranate

Fruit and nuts, uncooked or cooked by steaming or boiling in water, frozen, whether or not containing added sugar or other sweetening matter: IQF strawberries, frozen mango chunks, frozen mixed berries — all without other processing
Fruit and nuts, provisionally preserved (e.g., by sulphur dioxide gas, in brine, in sulphur water or in other preservative solutions) but unsuitable in that state for immediate consumption

Fruit, dried, other than that of headings 08.01 to 08.06; mixtures of nuts or dried fruits of this Chapter: prunes (dried plums), dried apricots, dried peaches, dried figs (classified here unlike fresh figs in 08.04), mixed dried fruit, trail mix of nuts and dried fruits

Peel of citrus fruit or of melons (including watermelons), fresh, frozen, dried or provisionally preserved in brine, in sulphur water or in other preservative solutions: citrus zest strips, candied peel BEFORE final sugaring process

exclusions.condition: Cooked, prepared or preserved fruits (jams, jellies, marmalades, fruit purées, fruit in sugar syrup, compotes)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 20: jams/marmalades → 20.07. Fruit in sugar syrup (canned peaches, canned mandarin segments) → 20.08. Fruit purée/paste (tomato paste → 20.02; fruit purée for baby food → 20.08). Dried fruit with added sugar coating → 20.08. Ch.08 = natural/minimal preservation only

exclusions.condition: Roasted, salted, sugared or flavoured nuts (processed beyond raw/dried)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 20.08 (nuts otherwise prepared or preserved). Roasted peanuts → 20.08. Salted macadamia → 20.08. Honey-roasted almonds → 20.08. Raw/plain dried nuts (regardless of species) → Ch.08

exclusions.condition: Oleaginous fruits (used primarily for oil extraction — groundnuts, olives, oil palm fruit, soybeans, sunflower seeds as agricultural commodity)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 12 (oil seeds and oleaginous fruits) when used primarily for oil: groundnuts (peanuts) as agricultural oil-seed commodity → 12.02. Olives (if for oil production in bulk) → borderline; table olives preserved → Ch.20; olive oil → Ch.15. Edible groundnuts for direct consumption → 08.02 or 12.02 depending on commercial presentation

exclusions.condition: Fruit juices, grape must, concentrated fruit juices, smoothies
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 20 (20.09): orange juice, apple juice, grape juice, tomato juice → 20.09. Fruit nectar → 20.09. Grape must for wine → 22.04. Whole/pieces of fruit are Ch.08; extracted juice is Ch.20/22

exclusions.condition: Peanut butter, almond butter, other nut pastes
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 20.08 (peanut butter/nut paste) — processed by grinding/roasting nuts into a paste → 20.08. Raw/shelled peanuts (unroasted, unprocessed) → 08.02 or 12.02

classification_rules.rule: Fresh vs. frozen vs. dried — all Ch.08 if simply preserved (not cooked, not sweetened with sugar as primary process)
classification_rules.description: Chapter 8 spans all MINIMAL PRESERVATION STATES for fruit/nuts: FRESH (living/just harvested). CHILLED (refrigerated, not frozen). FROZEN (08.11 — uncooked or steam/boiled for blanching only, may have added sweetener). DRIED (08.01–08.06 for specific fruits; 08.13 for other dried fruits — by sun, air, mechanical drying). PROVISIONALLY PRESERVED by gas/brine/sulphur water (08.12 — too harsh to eat directly but not cooked). The common characteristic: the FRUIT/NUT retains its natural identity. When transformed by cooking, sugaring substantially, or mixing with other food ingredients → Ch.20.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: raw/dried nuts (Ch.08) vs roasted nuts (Ch.20) vs nut oil (Ch.15) vs oilseed commodity (Ch.12)
classification_rules.description: Nuts appear across 4 chapters by processing level: RAW, DRIED (natural) → 08.01/08.02 (unroasted, uncoated, unprocessed — including plain shelled raw cashew, raw almond, raw walnut). ROASTED/FLAVOURED → 20.08 (any heat treatment beyond the nut's own drying, or any salt/flavour/sugar coating). NUT OIL (extracted) → Ch.15 (almond oil → 15.15; walnut oil → 15.15; peanut oil → 15.08). OILSEED COMMODITY (groundnuts/peanuts grown for oil industry, traded bulk before any processing) → 12.02. The processing threshold is whether the nut has been HEAT-TREATED (roasted) or COATED after harvest — if yes → Ch.20.

classification_rules.rule: Fresh figs vs dried figs: heading assignment differs
classification_rules.description: FRESH figs (and fresh dates, pineapples, avocados, guavas, mangoes, mangosteens) → 08.04. DRIED FIGS → 08.13 (NOT 08.04 — dried figs fall in the 'fruit dried, other' heading). This differs from grapes/raisins (08.06 covers both fresh grapes AND raisins). For consistent classification: fresh = 08.04/08.05/08.08/08.09/08.10 by species. Dried = 08.01 (coconuts/Brazil/cashew), 08.02 (other nuts), 08.03 (bananas), 08.06 (grapes/raisins), 08.13 (all other dried fruits including figs). Mixed dried fruit → 08.13.

classification_rules.rule: Coconut products: the fresh→copra→oil chain and classification at each stage
classification_rules.description: Coconut products span multiple chapters: FRESH COCONUT (whole, fresh, with husk/without husk, mature or immature drinking coconut) → 08.01. DESICCATED COCONUT (coconut flesh dried without oil extraction, for baking/cooking) → 08.01. COPRA (dried coconut meat for oil extraction, traded as oilseed commodity) → 12.03 (oilseeds/oleaginous fruits). COCONUT OIL (extracted) → 15.13. COCONUT MILK/CREAM (aqueous emulsion extracted from coconut flesh) → 20.08. COCONUT WATER (natural juice from fresh coconut) → borderline 08.01 or 20.09; if packaged as juice → 20.09. The key: whole/desiccated coconut flesh for food → 08.01. Traded for oil extraction → 12.03.

classification_rules.rule: 08.14 (citrus peel and melon rind) vs sugared/candied peel in Ch.20: the sugaring process boundary
classification_rules.description: Citrus peel and melon rind appear in Ch.08 and Ch.20: 08.14 = peel that is FRESH, FROZEN, DRIED or PROVISIONALLY PRESERVED only (not yet candied/sugared for confectionery use). CANDIED PEEL (boiled in sugar syrup, partially or fully crystallised with sugar) → 20.08. The sugaring/candying process is the boundary: peel simply dried or preserved with SO2/brine (still not edible directly as confectionery) → 08.14. Peel that has been processed into candied/glazed confectionery ingredient → 20.08. Fresh citrus zest strips (culinary use) → 08.14.
